title: Repository-native prompt compression with phase-local scheduling and forced divergent exploration
goal: Implement and evaluate a deterministic Prompt IR, invariant-hypergraph closure, query-conditioned coarse-to-fine compression, tokenizer accounting, forced-dreaming independent candidate exploration, quality-diversity archiving, verified rendering, and adversarial benchmarks so coding-agent prompts minimize token cost while widening useful mechanism coverage without weakening repository authority, exact commands, stop conditions, or evidence boundaries.
constraints: Compressed prompts and dream candidates remain derived projections; canonical authority and task capsules remain semantic owners
Mandatory invariant closure and task-local authority precede ranking, pruning, novelty, and selection
Token cost is measured per tokenizer, never inferred from characters
Divergent candidates are generated independently before convergence
Forced-dreaming benchmarks require at least eight candidates and five mechanism families
Surface rewording is not mechanism diversity
Dense peer coupling is an explicit ablation, not the default exploration topology
Negation, branch rules, exact argv, paths, SHAs, evidence classes, and stop conditions are protected
Symbolic, latent, embedding, hypervector, novelty, and diversity methods remain P0 until held-out evidence
No P0/P1/P2/P3 result promotes itself to P4
required_methods: Question-aware coarse selection with deterministic structural baseline and conditional-direction controls
Explicit position scheduling and position-sweep evaluation
Dynamic relevance-ranked budgets with closure and risk floors
Protected atom/span compression with optional contrastive task-conditioned scoring
Forced-dreaming envelope with sparse twelve-dimensional lens vectors
Independent mechanism-family explorers with no early ranking or candidate sharing
Mechanism-level duplicate rejection and diversity verification
Quality-diversity archive before fidelity-first Pareto integration
Factorial ablations isolating every compression and exploration component
success: At least 50 percent median token reduction versus canonical prose on declared tokenizers
Independent forced dreaming increases mechanism-family and effect-frontier coverage versus equal-compute repeated sampling
Quality-diversity archive preserves at least one valid candidate omitted by scalar top-k on a declared subset
Non-inferior execution behavior within a predeclared margin on available model runs
Exact-head repository tests and build pass
falsifiers: Any branch, worktree, authority, negation, exact-command, path, SHA, stop-condition, or evidence invariant is lost
Forced dreaming increases only prose length or lexical variation without changing mechanisms, proof obligations, tests, or selected plans
Independent forced dreaming fails to beat ordinary independent sampling at equal compute
Coupled explorers match or exceed independent explorers without diversity collapse on held-out tasks
Quality-diversity archiving provides no useful coverage beyond scalar selection
Symbolic or latent rendering increases tokens or execution variance without compensating benefit
Hypervector or latent retrieval introduces authority drift
Compression, exploration, recovery, or adjudication overhead exceeds saved inference and engineering value
The mathematical layer does not alter selection, proof obligations, verification, or candidate coverage
research_inputs: ACL 2026 Findings: Diversity Collapse in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
ACL 2026 Findings: Divergent Thinking - Escape the Homogeneity Trap
Chung et al., Physical Review X 8, 031003 (2018)
